steps.title: Use intercepts to shrink the choices
steps.content: The line of best fit crosses the vertical axis a little above $16{,}000$ when $t=0$. That eliminates the other choices immediately, because their intercepts are about $4{,}690$ and $9{,}060$. Only A and C have a reasonable intercept.

steps.title: Use a two-point calculation for the slope
steps.content: Use two readable points from the line, such as $(0,16000)$ and $(5,40000)$. The slope is approximately $$\frac{40000-16000}{5-0}=4800,$$ so the answer should be near $4{,}800$, not near $9{,}060$.

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: The matching choice, $n=16{,}090+4{,}680t$, has the right intercept and a slope close to the fitted slope. A choice with the right intercept but a slope almost twice as large. Therefore, A is correct.
